title: QUIC CRT server client-auth transcript/runtime prerequisite verification
preconditions: The Incursa.Quic library builds successfully on net10.0.
The internal server-auth transcript/runtime prerequisite is wired into the bridge and key schedule, and the public validator opens `ClientCertificateRequired` only on the callback-driven path.
The current client-only and exact-pinning CRT proofs remain available as regression references.

supplemental_sections.heading: Acceptance Criteria
supplemental_sections.content:
- The server flight emits exactly one CertificateRequest when the server-auth floor is enabled.
- The server-role transcript accepts client Certificate -> CertificateVerify -> Finished in that mode and fails closed when the client certificate callback rejects.
- The public validator accepts `ClientCertificateRequired` only on the callback-driven path and still rejects deferred chain/revocation customization on this slice.

status_summary: This verification artifact covers the public server `ClientCertificateRequired` gate and the internal callback-driven server-auth floor; broader chain/revocation customization remains deferred.
scope: Verify the public server `ClientCertificateRequired` gate and the server-role CertificateRequest / client certificate presentation transcript-runtime prerequisite while leaving broader chain/revocation customization deferred.
procedure: Run the new server-auth requirement-home tests once the bridge and transcript wiring exists.
Run the current CRT and API guards that must remain unchanged, especially the mainstream client TLS and exact-pinning floors.
Run the repo-local SpecTrace validation and markdown rendering checks after the canonical JSON updates.
expected_result:
- The public `ClientCertificateRequired` gate lands on the existing carrier.
- The internal server-auth prerequisite slice is proven with positive and negative requirement-home evidence.
- The mainstream client TLS path and exact-pinning path remain green.
- Transfer, retry, 0-RTT, key update, and broader PKI behavior remain unchanged.
